title: Muehlenbeckia australis
taxonomy: Muehlenbeckia australis is a plant species that belongs to the family Polygonaceae. This family consists of plants commonly known as buckwheat or smartweed. The genus Muehlenbeckia is a group of vines and shrubs native to Australia and New Zealand. Within the genus, Muehlenbeckia australis is further classified into the subfamily Polygonoideae. This classification suggests a close relationship with other plants in the Polygonoideae subfamily, which includes various species of buckwheat and smartweed.

intro: Muehlenbeckia australis, also known as Australian lace or climbing spinach, is a perennial vine native to Australia, belonging to the family Polygonaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and gastrointestinal problems, with its reported health benefits attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Muehlenbeckia australis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are thought to contribute to its therapeutic effects.
